hero.title: A New Path to GI Health
hero.subtitle: Contactless, uniform, depth-controlled laser ablation for Barrett's Esophagus — guided by imaging, designed for the standard endoscopy suite.
hero.imageAlt: Anatomical illustration showing EGAD target locations in the gastrointestinal tract

about.title: Transforming Gastroenterology Care
paragraphs: Digma Medical Ltd. is a medical technology company dedicated to transforming the treatment of gastroenterology pathologies, starting with Barrett's Esophagus (BE) — a pre-cancerous condition that may progress to esophageal adenocarcinoma, one of the most deadly forms of GI cancer.

We are developing a minimally invasive therapy, the Endoscopic Gastrointestinal Ablation Device (EGAD), that provides contactless, uniform, deep ablation guided by imaging. EGAD is designed to treat the full circumference of the esophagus or isolated patches in a single, safe, and efficient procedure — targeting mucosal tissue while protecting deeper layers such as the muscularis.

Our product consists of a console and a single-use catheter compatible with the working channel of standard gastrointestinal endoscopes. The EGAD procedure is being developed for performance in a standard GI endoscopy suite without requiring additional imaging equipment.
The EGAD procedure is currently being evaluated in clinical studies.
about.clinicalCta: To learn more about our clinical program, please contact us.

barretts.title: Barrett's Esophagus
conditionPoints: Caused by chronic GERD (gastroesophageal reflux disease)
Often progresses silently and is diagnosed too late
Symptoms include heartburn, regurgitation, and difficulty swallowing
Increases risk of esophageal adenocarcinoma (EAC) by up to 125×
Ablation eliminates abnormal tissue and enables regeneration
High recurrence with current therapies often requires multiple sessions
Rising demand for effective, safe, and minimally invasive solutions
solutionPoints: Precision ablation with controlled depth and energy — Map & Mark before Ablate
"One and Done" — single tool, multi-zone treatment
Effective ablation without full tissue contact
Shorter procedure; potential to avoid general anesthesia
barretts.imageAlt: Endoscopic view of Barrett's esophagus showing abnormal salmon-colored mucosa
barretts.imageCaption: Barrett's esophagus — pre-cancerous change in the esophageal lining, often linked to chronic GERD.

technology.title: EGAD Technology
technology.subtitle: Minimally invasive LASER-based balloon-catheter delivery
technology.imageAlt: EGAD treatment console with through-the-scope laser delivery system
technology.imageCaption: The EGAD console and single-use catheter — designed for the standard GI endoscopy suite.
technology.secondaryImageAlt: EGAD single-use catheter in sterile packaging

features.title: Through-the-scope
features.description: Single-use catheter delivered via the working channel of standard GI endoscopes.

features.title: Contactless ablation
features.description: Uniform, depth-controlled treatment without requiring full tissue contact.

features.title: Balloon-centered laser
features.description: Balloon system centralizes the laser with catheter anchoring for stable delivery.

features.title: Dual-wavelength guidance
features.description: Visible red guide pointer for targeting; NIR therapeutic laser for ablation.

features.title: Standard GI suite
features.description: Outpatient procedure using standard endoscopy technique — no fluoroscopy required.

features.title: Selective interaction
features.description: Adjustable tissue interaction designed for various anatomies and treatment zones.

market.title: Market Opportunity
market.subtitle: Global Barrett's Esophagus ablation devices market
market.footnote: Large unmet need and high recurrence rates with current therapies continue to drive demand for safer, more durable ablation solutions.
